evals.prompt: I need a detailed cost breakdown for adding cross-save to our Unity game. Saves currently live in a custom JSON system, and we want Steam Cloud on PC plus a migration path for existing local saves. Please investigate the repo first and give me the effort, risks, and phased work rather than implementation.
evals.expected_output: An evidence-backed costing report that investigates the codebase first, accounts for migration risk and external service setup, and produces phased estimates instead of implementation work.
expectations: Triggers on costing-oriented Unity planning request
Launches multiple parallel explore agents before estimating
Uses a librarian agent because Steam Cloud or external service setup affects scope
Loads investigation-workflow.md, sizing-guide.md, and risk-assessment.md from unity-standards
Produces an overall size, total hour range, and confidence level
Breaks work into epics and tasks with cost sizes and hour ranges
Includes assumptions, risks, and migration considerations
Cites file:line evidence for architecture or risk claims
Does not call task_create
Does not implement the feature

evals.prompt: Can you price a major refactor of our Unity inventory system? I want a detailed estimate for moving from tightly coupled MonoBehaviours to a cleaner event-driven setup, including test work, rollout order, and what the critical path looks like.
evals.expected_output: A detailed costing report for a cross-system refactor with investigation, risk analysis, task sequencing, and critical path guidance.
expectations: Focuses on costing only, not implementation planning for execution
Investigates boundaries, dependencies, side effects, and tests before estimating
Uses shared sizing guidance instead of ad hoc size labels
Identifies critical path and sequencing dependencies
Prices test and stabilization work explicitly
Uses ranges rather than false precision
Surfaces out-of-scope items or assumptions when needed

evals.prompt: What would it cost to add ranked matchmaking to our Unity project? We already use FishNet for networking and are considering PlayFab for backend services. I need a proper effort estimate with discovery work if the unknowns are too large.
evals.expected_output: A costing report that investigates the local networking architecture, accounts for PlayFab/FishNet external uncertainty, and recommends a discovery spike if confidence is low.
expectations: Triggers on Unity effort-estimate request even without the word costing plan
Uses explore agents to inspect the local networking codebase
Uses librarian for FishNet or PlayFab constraints and setup overhead
Includes a low-confidence path with a priced discovery spike when justified
Provides epic and task costing rather than a single total
Includes risks, assumptions, and recommended phases

evals.prompt: Estimate the work to add invincibility frames and a hit-flash effect to enemies in our Unity action game. This should stay inside the local combat code; no new SDKs or third-party services are involved. I want hours, risks, and a short task breakdown.
evals.expected_output: A local-only Unity costing report that investigates the combat codebase, produces evidence-backed hours and risks, and does not involve external-doc research.
expectations: Triggers on Unity estimate request without requiring the word costing
Uses explore agents to inspect the local combat-related code
Does not use librarian because no external package, SDK, or service affects the estimate
Provides a task breakdown with hour ranges and confidence
Includes risks and assumptions without switching into implementation

evals.prompt: Plan the architecture for our new Unity dialogue system and suggest a clean folder structure. I do not need an estimate yet; I just want the design approach.
evals.expected_output: The skill should not trigger because this is a design/planning request without estimate intent.
expectations: Does not trigger on generic Unity planning without estimate intent
